commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR updates hosted shell, CLI shell, and PowerShell installers to choose npm freshness bypass arguments from raw npm config files, adds regression coverage, and records a changelog fix.

PR surface: Tests +295, Docs +1, Other +74. Total +370 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. The PR body gives a real npm 11.12.1 reproduction, npm's source shows `before` and `min-release-age` are exclusive, and current/PR source inspection shows the installer flag-selection path.
commands.review_followup: The remaining blocker is a narrow mechanical compatibility repair in the shell resolvers plus focused tests, so it is suitable for a repair lane if maintainers want automation to patch this PR.

target.title: fix: handle npm min-release-age in installers
target.body:
Summary
- Replays #84749 on current main because the contributor fork branch is no longer maintainer-writable and has become conflicted.
- Keeps npm global installer freshness bypasses compatible with npm versions that reject a computed min-release-age config together with --before.
- Adds shell, CLI shell, PowerShell, and npm env regression coverage plus a changelog entry crediting @TeodoroRodrigo.

Verification
- git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
- bash -n scripts/install.sh scripts/install-cli.sh
- fnm exec --using v24.15.0 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/install-sh.test.ts test/scripts/install-cli.test.ts test/scripts/install-ps1.test.ts src/infra/npm-install-env.test.ts
- npm 11.12.1 repro: install with --before fails when raw min-release-age=7 exists; install with --min-release-age=0 succeeds
- .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main

Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: npm global installs fail when older npm derives before from raw min-release-age config and the hosted installer also passes --before.
Real environment tested: local macOS checkout with npm 11.12.1 and isolated HOME/.npmrc containing min-release-age=7.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: HOME=<tmp-home> npm --before=2026-05-24T00:00:00.000Z install -g <tmp-pkg> --prefix <tmp-prefix> --no-audit --no-fund, then HOME=<tmp-home> npm --min-release-age=0 install -g <tmp-pkg> --prefix <tmp-prefix> --no-audit --no-fund.
Evidence after fix: --before path exits 1 with "--min-release-age cannot be provided when using --before"; --min-release-age=0 path exits 0 and installs the package.
Observed result after fix: installers choose --min-release-age=0 when raw min-release-age config is present, and choose --before only for before-only policies.
What was not tested: full hosted installer download from public CDN; GitHub CI covers installer sync and cross-platform checks.
target.author: steipete
